strengths_deep_dive: Islamic Oasis Center CO stands out for its institutional longevity and financial resilience. Having <cite id="10">operated since 2008</cite>, it has built a track record that spans over 15 years. Its financial health is characterized by a massive reserve, with <cite id="11">net assets reaching $5,726,976</cite> by the end of 2023. This growth is supported by a <cite id="12">100% independent board</cite>, which provides critical oversight for its multi-million dollar budget. The organization's ability to scale is evident in its revenue jump from <cite id="13">$4.3 million in 2021 to over $6 million in 2023</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2008, Islamic Oasis Center CO has evolved into a significant humanitarian presence based in Colorado, focusing on <cite id="1">monthly food pantry services and life skills support</cite> for low-income and refugee families. The organization manages a substantial operation with <cite id="2">$6,039,144 in total revenue</cite> as of the 2023 tax year. While its roots are domestic, the charity's mission extends to <cite id="3">emergency response and medical support</cite> for vulnerable populations. The organization maintains an exceptionally strong financial cushion, reporting <cite id="4">17.2 months of working capital</cite>, which provides a high degree of long-term stability. However, the financial data presents a complex picture. The <cite id="5">program expense ratio is reported at 4.7%</cite>, a figure that is significantly lower than the <cite id="6">peer program ratio median of 90.3%</cite>. This suggests that the organization's current expense breakdown may be incomplete in public filings or that it is in a phase of significant asset accumulation rather than immediate disbursement. Despite this, the charity demonstrates high fundraising efficiency, reporting <cite id="7">$0.00 spent to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. For Muslim donors, the center provides a <cite id="8">verified zakat-eligible pathway</cite> to support the 'masakin' (needy) through direct service delivery.
